stories.summary: US president also expresses hope ‘something will happen’ on Ukraine after ‘productive’ conversations with Zelenskyy and Putin, after Emmanuel Macron praised ‘real progress’ on the war at summitRutte says the adjustment in the US pledge to the Nato Force Model is “not primarily about where forces and assets are currently, but about who would do what if our defence plans were activated.”He says historically the model was “overly reliant” on the US.

stories.headline: Trump accuses Obama of ‘bribing’ Iran in 2015 nuclear deal at G7 summit
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has accused his predecessor Barack Obama of ‘bribing’ Iran to agree to the 2015 nuclear deal.

stories.headline: Israel continues strikes on Lebanon ahead of anticipated US-Iran framework deal
stories.summary: While violence has declined in Lebanon since a US-Iran agreement to end the Middle East war was announced on Monday, Israeli strikes on the south have killed at least five people since the deal, according to Lebanese state media.

stories.headline: Trump Says Iran Deal to Be Signed Tomorrow or Friday
stories.summary: "The deal we reached with Iran on Sunday will be signed shortly, tomorrow, maybe the next day,” President Donald Trump said during a press conference at the G7 summit in France.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Corn Ships Cross Hormuz Toward Iran, UAE Ahead of Trump Deal
stories.summary: A fully-loaded corn ship bound for Iran entered the Persian Gulf after crossing the US blockade line outside the Strait of Hormuz, ahead of the US-Iran interim peace deal aimed at reopening the waterway.

stories.summary: President Trump denied that the United States would be part of a $300 billion rebuilding fund for Tehran and argued that his agreement was better than the one Barack Obama struck in 2015.

stories.headline: Israeli air strikes on Lebanon continue despite US-Iran deal
stories.summary: There has been a reduction in violence since the US-Iran agreement was announced, but attacks have not stopped.

stories.headline: Bus with Belarus children's football team hit by a drone in Russia, Moscow claims
stories.summary: A bus carrying a children's football team from Belarus in Russia's border Bryansk region was hit by a drone, according to Russian officials. Moscow claims it was attacked by Ukraine, but Kyiv denies it carried out any operations in the area.

stories.headline: Rutte heads to Washington to smooth NATO tensions ahead of July summit
stories.summary: EXCLUSIVE: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte is travelling to Washington next Tuesday ahead of the annual NATO summit in Ankara in July. Relations between the US and its NATO allies have been strained after Trump accused European nations of abandoning the US during its war in Iran.

stories.headline: World Environment Day summit demands accelerated global action on climate crisis
stories.summary: With air pollution claiming 520,000 lives annually in the region, global leaders gathered in Baku for World Environment Day to demand accelerated action against interconnected health and climate crises.

stories.headline: Equatorial Guinea government resigns after failing to meet targets
stories.summary: The vice-president says the government had barely reached 10% of its targets, without specifying them.

stories.headline: SpaceX Shares Fall for First Time Since Blockbuster Debut
stories.summary: SpaceX shares declined for the first time since its record initial public offering, snapping a three-day rally that vaulted it past Amazon.com Inc. as the world’s fifth-largest stock.

stories.headline: US Oil Stockpiles Fall to Critical Lows as War Spurs Exports
stories.summary: Inventories at the US’s largest commercial crude oil storage hub fell to the lowest since 2014, as surging exports during the Iran war drain supplies in America.

stories.headline: Starmer’s Hot Mic Moment Scoops UK-India Trade Announcement
stories.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and his Indian counterpart Narendra Modi inadvertently revealed their two countries had resolved a trade impasse when they were overheard at the Group of Seven summit saying an agreement had been reached.

stories.headline: Takeaways From the Runoff and Primary Elections in Georgia, Alabama and Oklahoma
stories.summary: Lt. Gov. Burt Jones lost the Republican runoff for governor to the health care executive Rick Jackson despite the president’s endorsement. Mr. Trump’s picks won in other races.

stories.summary: Trump-endorsed candidates won Senate runoffs in Georgia and Alabama, but in a rare defeat, the president’s pick for Georgia governor failed to advance.

stories.headline: Trump jokes ‘I’m the boss’ as he arrives for G7 meeting
stories.summary: Donald Trump joked that he was “the boss” as he arrived for a G7 meeting in France on Wednesday. The light-hearted remark came before leaders gathered for talks on trade, economic challenges and global financial stability alongside representatives from partner nations.

stories.headline: Leader of South Africa's second biggest party wants his predecessor sacked as minister
stories.summary: John Steenhuisen is one of South Africa's best-known politicians after leading the DA for seven years.

stories.summary: Brazilian Nickel Ltd. is seeking an anchor investor to help attract more equity funding for its planned $1.4 billion nickel and cobalt mine in northeastern Brazil.

stories.summary: National Stock Exchange of India Ltd., the operator of the world’s busiest derivatives market, has filed draft documents for what’s on track to be one of the largest initial public offerings in India’s history.

stories.summary: Chile’s Antofagasta Plc has approached Chinese copper smelters with a proposal to price contractual sales of copper ore using spot-market indexes, a fresh sign that a decades-old system based on fixed-price deals is under strain.

stories.summary: Rene Mouawad Airport to serve Istanbul, Dubai, and Mersin as part of plans to revitalise northern Lebanon's economy.

stories.headline: Drug monopoly extension splits EU ministers in Biotech Act debate
stories.summary: EU health ministers split over a proposal to give certain innovative medicines an extra year of market protection, with EU Health Commissioner arguing the incentive is needed to keep biotech investment in Europe. Incoming Irish presidency, in the meantime, says it will work as quickly as possible.

stories.headline: Afghan government staff told to abandon smartphones or face prison sentence
stories.summary: How widely the rule is being implemented across Afghanistan and the scale of its impact were not entirely clear.

stories.headline: Intense heat on the way, but no alarmism: IPMA rejects exaggerated forecasts
stories.summary: Portugal's weather agency says Lisbon and inland areas will see very high temperatures next week, with yellow or orange alerts likely, but dismisses alarmist online forecasts.

stories.headline: Cuba tourism collapses as US pressure campaign bites
stories.summary: The number of foreign visitors is down by 58% compared to last year, Cuban officials say, amid sanctions and an effective oil blockade.

stories.summary: Wall Street traders gearing up for the Federal Reserve decision refrained from making big moves as they waited to see whether new Chair Kevin Warsh will validate bets on future rate hikes or lean against them.

stories.headline: Oil Rebounds as Traders Await Signing of US-Iran Deal
stories.summary: Oil recovered after two days of stark declines, with prices still broadly pressured by a pending US-Iran interim peace deal that could send barrels gushing back onto the market.
